mechanism_narrative: High unemployment signals significant economic slack, suppressing wage inflation and corporate costs. This boosts profit margins and leads to positive equity returns as the market anticipates a policy response and eventual recovery.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is above 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate, near the zero lower bound, provides a powerful tailwind for equities by lowering the discount rate for future cash flows and encouraging risk-taking behavior among investors seeking yield.
failure_condition: The correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) during periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of accelerating inflation and decelerating industrial production signals stagflationary pressure. This dynamic erodes corporate profit margins through rising input costs and weakening demand, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0.5% AND 3-month change in INDPRO < -0.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in over 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The rate of change in retail sales is a direct measure of consumer spending momentum. Positive momentum in sales translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts is a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, as construction has significant multiplier effects on employment and consumption. This signals future economic weakness and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months following a HOUST reading below 900K.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment, strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a robust recovery is underway. This positive surprise boosts investor confidence and expectations for future corporate earnings, leading to short-term positive returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6%, the average 5-day S&P 500 return following a PAYEMS print > 200K is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter change in real GDP indicates a contraction in the overall economy. This directly implies lower aggregate corporate earnings and reduced investor appetite for risk, leading to negative forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The sign of the quarterly change in GDPC1 fails to predict the sign of the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return with greater than 50% accuracy over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low federal funds rate and high unemployment signals that monetary policy is highly accommodative and will likely remain so for an extended period. This 'Fed put' provides strong support for equity valuations.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return when the trigger condition is met is not statistically significantly greater than the unconditional average return.
